compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Hi-Fi Rush
- Steam AppID of the game: 1817230

## System Information
- GPU: AMD 5700XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 22.3.3
- Kernel version: 6.1.8
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/alosarjos/aae849ae851caa304c52305ebe5df1a1
- Proton version: Proton Experimental

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Sound mutes from time to time for a second or two, then it comes back.


## Reproduction

Just playing the game, I haven't found any specific time between the mutes or a way to reproduce it manually. I'm suing Pipewire 0.3.64.

I was looking the journal logs to see if there was something, all I found it this:

```log
ene 29 21:24:06 artorias kernel: umip_printk: 3 callbacks suppressed
ene 29 21:24:06 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:14ac47523 sp:b9edd0: SGDT instruction cannot be used by applications.
ene 29 21:24:06 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:14ac47523 sp:b9edd0: For now, expensive software emulation returns the result.
ene 29 21:24:06 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:150e8121f sp:b9e930: SGDT instruction cannot be used by applications.
ene 29 21:24:06 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:150e8121f sp:b9e930: For now, expensive software emulation returns the result.
ene 29 21:24:06 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:150e8121f sp:b9e930: SGDT instruction cannot be used by applications.
ene 29 21:27:11 artorias kernel: umip_printk: 7 callbacks suppressed
ene 29 21:27:11 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:1524d4b95 sp:b9f058: SGDT instruction cannot be used by applications.
ene 29 21:27:11 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:1524d4b95 sp:b9f058: For now, expensive software emulation returns the result.
ene 29 21:27:11 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:1524d4b95 sp:b9f058: SGDT instruction cannot be used by applications.
ene 29 21:27:11 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:1524d4b95 sp:b9f058: For now, expensive software emulation returns the result.
ene 29 21:27:11 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110765] ip:148623d7e sp:66ccf698: SGDT instruction cannot be used by applications.
ene 29 21:33:15 artorias kernel: umip_printk: 13 callbacks suppressed
ene 29 21:33:15 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:1562a8272 sp:b9e2c8: SGDT instruction cannot be used by applications.
ene 29 21:33:15 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:1562a8272 sp:b9e2c8: For now, expensive software emulation returns the result.
ene 29 21:33:52 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:1524d4b95 sp:b9f058: SGDT instruction cannot be used by applications.
ene 29 21:33:52 artorias kernel: umip: Hi-Fi-RUSH.exe[110565] ip:1524d4b95 sp:b9f058: For now, expensive software emulation returns the result.
```

This messages repeat a lot. Since it's the only game I've seen this happening I'm guessing it's something related to Proton and not Pipewire or my sistem

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Door Kickers 2
- Steam AppID of the game: 1239080

## System Information
- GPU: RX 6700 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 22.0.5-1 and LLVM 1:14.0.4-4
- Kernel version: 5.17.0-3-amd64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/physixcat/e61d375f0a3ebaebc70ff0368f745f9f):
- Proton version: Proton Experimental (so newer than 7.0-2), have also tried older ones

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Proton Log:
[steam-1239080.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8883214/steam-1239080.log)

## Symptoms
Since the SWAT update (v0.31), playing missions causes the game to slowly use more and more RAM (even when the game is paused to the menu or in the results screen). In ~20 minutes, it can go up to 2.6 GB of RAM of usage, and I've seen it run away up to 8 GB before I killed it. Previous to this update, it never seemed to ever go over 1 GB of RAM throughout long play sessions.

## Reproduction
1. Use any version of Proton, I'm using Experimental here (newer than 7.0-2, doesn't seem to matter though tried several, even the unofficial latest GE Proton release 7-20)
2. Start a program analogous to Task Manager (like bpytop or htop) that can track RAM usage of specific processes 
3. Start the game, run a mission (any will do, but for the purpose of standardizing let's go with Gate Crasher, the first non-tutorial ranger mission)
4. Complete the mission, then stay in the results (stars) screen for a couple minutes. The RAM usage continues to increase. For reference, a normal value here previous to SWAT update seemed to be ~850megs.
symptom_summary:
用户在玩《Door Kickers 2》时，游戏在进行任务时会逐渐消耗越来越多的内存，即使在暂停或结果屏幕中也会继续增加，最终内存使用量可达8GB。此问题自SWAT更新（v0.31）后出现，之前的内存使用量从未超过1GB。

**Symptom Tags:** Memory Leak, High RAM Usage, Performance Issue

issue_title: Call of Duty®: Black Ops Cold War (1985810)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Call of Duty®: Black Ops Cold War
- Steam AppID of the game: 1985810

## System Information
- GPU: <!-- e.g. RX 580 or GTX 970 --> Steam Deck
- Driver/LLVM version: <!-- e.g. Mesa 18.2/7.0.0 or nvidia 396.54 --> Mesa 22.2.0.157821.radeonsi_3.4.0_2-2
- Kernel version: <!-- e.g. 4.17 --> 5.13.0-valve36-1-neptune
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Parapass/b3b58bdd1b1be226e9d5dd840e9f0c2d
- Proton version: Proton 7.0-6

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
[steam-1985810.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/11764589/steam-1985810.log)


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
The game launches in Steam and is listed as playing but does not start at all.

Using journalctl I saw that the game was trying to find d3d12core.dll in /home/deck/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/1985810/pfx/drive_c/windows/system32/ and /home/deck/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/1985810/pfx/drive_c/windows/syswow64/ but could not find it as the file did not exist. Would this be a Wine problem or a Proton problem? On my Windows 10 install I can find both d3d12.dll and d3d12core.dll

## Reproduction
Always happens

compatibility_report:
Marvel's Spider-Man 2 (2651280)

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/8512.
@DistantThunder posted on 2025-03-10T12:57:46:

# Compatibility Report

- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Marvel’s SpiderMan 2 with Raytracing
- Steam AppID of the game: 2651280

## System Information

- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX
- Video driver version: Mesa 24.3.4-arch1.1 (amdgpu)
- Kernel version: 6.13.2-zen1-1-zen
- Proton version: Experimental / 9.0.4


## I confirm:

- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.
- [x] I have verified the game & Proton's files. 

## Symptoms

Marvel’s SpiderMan 2 previously supported Raytracing, but after an update around two weeks ago, enabling Raytracing no longer works. The game displays a Wine error: `"status & "&"vkCreateRayTracingPipelinesKHR""`. Other games using Raytracing are unaffected.

_The game *can* work with Raytracing disabled but it removes significant visual fidelity._

* Game version: v1.307.0.0


## Reproduction

1. Ensure the latest updates for your system (kernel, drivers) have been applied.
2. Launch Marvel’s SpiderMan 2 with Proton Experimental version or 9.0.4 via Steam.
3. Enable Raytracing in-game settings or just start the game with Raytracing previously enabled.
4. Observe if the error message appears.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f3aa6070-c04f-4e61-9483-bc895f42f46e)

### Additional Context

- **Logs:** I have enabled `PROTON_LOG=1` for debugging but the log file is now 400 MB and the game still hasn't started.
- The issue started occurring after an update to the game most probably, approximately 1 week ago or less.

Please let me know if there is additional information needed. Thank you for your assistance in resolving this issue.
symptom_summary:
用户在更新后发现《Marvel's Spider-Man 2》的光线追踪功能无法启用，游戏显示Wine错误信息，且禁用光线追踪后视觉效果显著下降。 

Tags: Ray Tracing Issue, Visual Glitches, Error Messages

issue_title: Proton won't launch any games using NVIDIA 390 drivers
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Everything I've tried; so far A Raven Monologue, Terraforming Mars, and Ticket to Ride
- Steam AppID of the game: 744810, 800270, 108200

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce GT 650M
- Driver/LLVM version: nvidia-driver-390 390.144-0ubuntu0.20.04.1
- Kernel version: 5.13.0-30-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/mark-raymond/971ec8b3079b46d9ab3b764aa0fb4b82
- Proton version: I have mostly tested using 7.0-1, but also tried experimental (7.0-20220218b) and 6.3-8

## I confirm:
- ~[ ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.~ N/A, this is not game specific
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

```
======================
Proton: 1644626092 proton-7.0-1
SteamGameId: 744810
Command: ['/home/mark/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/A Raven Monologue/A Raven Monologue.exe']
Options: {'forcelgadd'}
depot: 0.20220131.43
pressure-vessel: 0.20220131.0
scripts: v0.20211029.0-0-g2d89be4
soldier: 0.20220131.0 soldier 0.20220131.0
Kernel: Linux 5.13.0-30-generic #33~20.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Mon Feb 7 14:25:10 UTC 2022 x86_64
======================
```
Other games result in an identical log, with only the game ID and command changing.

## Symptoms

When I run any game:
- I press play
- This dialogue appears:
  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2951053/155016421-8e2e5864-88e9-4982-8ff9-50b16ad3a9a1.png)
- After a few seconds, it disappears. The play button is now a stop button.
- A few seconds later, the stop button returns to being a play button.

## Reproduction

Attempt to run any game using Proton. Obviously this works for most people, so I feel a bit silly filing this bug report, but every other resource I've found with these symptoms has been due to people using NTFS, and I'm not using NTFS, I'm was using BTRFS, and I tried ext4 as well.
symptom_summary:

**Tags:** White Screen, Unresponsive, Launch Issue
